content.problemStatement:
Eren, Mikasa, and Armin are playing a game with an array AAA of length NNN to get FREEDOM.

Eren can select any indices iii and jjj such that 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1\le i\lt j\le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N.

Let a,b,a, b,a,b, and ccc denote the points of Eren, Mikasa, and Armin respectively, where:


a=Ai‚àíAja = A_i - A_ja=Ai‚Äã‚àíAj‚Äã
b=Ai+Ajb = A_i + A_jb=Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã
c=Ai√óAjc = A_i \times A_jc=Ai‚Äã√óAj‚Äã
Eren, Mikasa and Armin will get FREEDOM if the values of a,b,a, b,a,b, and ccc form an arithmetic progression.

The task is to count the number of ways Eren can select pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that they get FREEDOM.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that Eren, Mikasa and Armin get FREEDOM.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚ãÖ1055\cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are no pairs that satisfy the condition.
Test case 222: There is one pair possible :

(4,5)(4,5)(4,5) : a=(6‚àí2)=4,b=(6+2)=8,c=(6√ó2)=12a=(6-2)=4,b=(6+2)=8,c=(6\times 2)=12a=(6‚àí2)=4,b=(6+2)=8,c=(6√ó2)=12. We can see that a,b,ca,b,ca,b,c are in AP.
